sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content:
Systemic therapy is indicated for advanced, metastatic, or unresectable hepatobiliary cancers. Treatment selection depends on the primary tumor site (HCC vs. BTC), underlying liver function, performance status, and biomarker status (e.g., FGFR2 fusions, IDH1 mutations, MSI-H/dMMR for BTC). For HCC, targeted therapy and immunotherapy have become standard. For BTC, platinum-based chemotherapy is first-line, with targeted therapies for specific molecular alterations.

By Stage and Setting:
- HCC First-line: Atezolizumab plus bevacizumab (IMbrave150). Alternatives: sorafenib or lenvatinib.
- HCC Second-line: Regorafenib, cabozantinib, ramucirumab (if AFP ≥400), or pembrolizumab/nivolumab.
- BTC First-line: Gemcitabine plus cisplatin (ABC-02). Alternatives: GEMOX or fluoropyrimidine-based. For MSI-H/dMMR: pembrolizumab.
- BTC Second-line: Fluoropyrimidine-based (FOLFOX, capecitabine). Targeted: futibatinib for FGFR2 fusions, ivosidenib for IDH1 mutations.
- BTC Adjuvant: Capecitabine for 6 months (BILCAP).

Key Regimens:
1. Atezolizumab plus Bevacizumab: First-line advanced HCC. Drugs: Atezolizumab 1200 mg IV Day 1, Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg IV Day 1. 21-day cycle. Toxicity: Hypertension, proteinuria, diarrhea, hypothyroidism, hepatitis, colitis, rash.
2. Sorafenib: First-line alternative or second-line. 400 mg PO BID continuous. Toxicity: Hand-foot skin reaction, diarrhea, hypertension, fatigue.
3. Lenvatinib: First-line alternative. 8 mg (if <60 kg) or 12 mg (if ≥60 kg) PO daily. Toxicity: Hypertension, diarrhea, decreased appetite, weight loss, proteinuria.
4. Gemcitabine plus Cisplatin: First-line advanced BTC. Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 IV Days 1&8, Cisplatin 25 mg/m2 IV Days 1&8. 21-day cycle. Toxicity: Myelosuppression, nausea, vomiting, fatigue, nephrotoxicity.
5. Capecitabine (Adjuvant BTC): 1250 mg/m2 PO BID Days 1-14, 21-day cycle x 8 cycles. Toxicity: Hand-foot syndrome, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, myelosuppression.
6. Futibatinib: Previously treated ICC with FGFR2 fusion. 20 mg PO daily continuous. Toxicity: Hyperphosphatemia, stomatitis, dry eye, fatigue, diarrhea.

cards.name: Atezolizumab plus Bevacizumab
cards.detail: Setting: First-line advanced HCC. Drugs: Atezolizumab 1200 mg IV Day 1, Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg IV Day 1. Cycle: 21-day cycle. Key toxicities: Hypertension, proteinuria, diarrhea, hypothyroidism, hepatitis, colitis, rash.

cards.name: Sorafenib
cards.detail: Setting: First-line advanced HCC (alternative) or second-line after atezolizumab/bevacizumab. Drugs: Sorafenib 400 mg Oral Twice daily. Cycle: Continuous daily dosing. Key toxicities: Hand-foot skin reaction, diarrhea, hypertension, fatigue, weight loss.

cards.name: Lenvatinib
cards.detail: Setting: First-line advanced HCC (alternative). Drugs: Lenvatinib 8 mg (for weight <60 kg) or 12 mg (for weight ≥60 kg) Oral Once daily. Cycle: Continuous daily dosing. Key toxicities: Hypertension, diarrhea, decreased appetite, weight loss, proteinuria, palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia.

cards.name: Gemcitabine plus Cisplatin
cards.detail: Setting: First-line advanced BTC. Drugs: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 IV Days 1 and 8, Cisplatin 25 mg/m2 IV Days 1 and 8. Cycle: 21-day cycle. Key toxicities: Myelosuppression (neutropenia, thrombocytopenia), nausea, vomiting, fatigue, nephrotoxicity.

cards.name: Capecitabine (Adjuvant for BTC)
cards.detail: Setting: Adjuvant after curative-intent resection of BTC. Drugs: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 Oral Twice daily on days 1-14. Cycle: 21-day cycle, for 8 cycles (6 months). Key toxicities: Hand-foot syndrome, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, myelosuppression, hyperbilirubinemia.

cards.name: Futibatinib
cards.detail: Setting: Previously treated locally advanced or metastatic ICC with FGFR2 fusion/insertion. Drugs: Futibatinib 20 mg Oral Once daily. Cycle: Continuous daily dosing. Key toxicities: Hyperphosphatemia, stomatitis, dry eye, fatigue, diarrhea, palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia.

timeline.title: 1st Line - HCC
timeline.body: Preferred: Atezolizumab plus bevacizumab. Alternatives: Sorafenib or lenvatinib.

timeline.title: 2nd Line - HCC
timeline.body: Options: regorafenib, cabozantinib, ramucirumab (if AFP ≥400), or pembrolizumab/nivolumab.

timeline.title: 1st Line - BTC
timeline.body: Preferred: Gemcitabine plus cisplatin. Alternatives: GEMOX or fluoropyrimidine-based. For MSI-H/dMMR: pembrolizumab.

timeline.title: 2nd Line - BTC
timeline.body: Fluoropyrimidine-based chemotherapy (FOLFOX, capecitabine). Targeted: futibatinib for FGFR2 fusions, ivosidenib for IDH1 mutations.
